title: oxalis jasminifolia
intro: Oxalis jasminifolia, also known as the Jasmine Violet or Violet Woodsorrel, is a flowering plant species in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). It is a perennial plant with heart-shaped leaves and delicate, fragrant flowers that resemble jasmine. The leaves of the Oxalis jasminifolia plant contain oxalic acid, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. Oxalis jasminifolia is used in herbal preparations to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations, and is often consumed as a tea or taken in capsule form.
